# Worth AI — Redshift & PostgreSQL Schema Reference

<!-- Auto-generated by sync_redshift_schema.py on 2026-04-09 19:45 UTC -->
<!-- Source: confirmed schemas (no live connection) -->

## How to read this file

- **PostgreSQL tables** (port 5432): `rds_warehouse_public.*`, `rds_cases_public.*`, `integration_data.*`, `core.*`
- **Redshift tables** (port 5439): `datascience.*`, `zoominfo.*`, `warehouse.*`, `ml_model.*`
- **Confirmed JSON pattern (Redshift)**: `JSON_EXTRACT_PATH_TEXT(value, 'key')` — works because `value` is `VARCHAR`, not `JSONB`
- **What fails on Redshift**: `value::jsonb`, `value->>'key'` — both require JSONB type, which doesn't exist on Redshift
- **Python extraction**: `json.loads(value_str).get('value')` — always works regardless of database type
- **similarity_index**: integer 0–55; `confidence = similarity_index / 55.0` (MAX_CONFIDENCE_INDEX=55 defined in sources.ts)
- **Platform IDs**: 16=Middesk, 23=OpenCorporates, 24=ZoomInfo, 17=Equifax, 38=Trulioo, 31=AI-enrichment, 22=SERP, 40=Plaid

---

## Confirmed Tables (fallback — no live connection)

*8 tables from confirmed schemas. Run with credentials to get full live discovery.*

### `rds_warehouse_public.facts`

> Central fact store — one row per (business_id, fact_name). value column is VARCHAR JSON: {value, source:{platformId,confidence}, alternatives:[], override:{value,userId}}. CONFIRMED WORKING: SELECT name, value, received_at FROM rds_warehouse_public.facts WHERE business_id='uuid' AND name IN ('naics_code','mcc_code');

| # | Column | Type | Nullable | Notes |
|---|--------|------|----------|-------|
| 1 | `id` | BIGINT | N |  |
| 2 | `business_id` | VARCHAR | N | UUID — joins across all tables |
| 3 | `name` | VARCHAR | N |  |
| 4 | `value` | VARCHAR | Y | VARCHAR containing JSON on both PostgreSQL and Redshift — use JSON_EXTRACT_PATH_TEXT(value,'key') on Redshift; json.loads(value) in Python |
| 5 | `received_at` | TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE | Y | Timestamp when the fact was last written/updated |

---

### `datascience.customer_files`

> Pipeline B output (batch, Redshift-only). Winner rule: WHEN zi_match_confidence > efx_match_confidence THEN zi_c_naics6 ELSE efx_primnaicscode. OC/Middesk/Trulioo/Liberty/AI are EXCLUDED from Pipeline B. primary_naics_code=NULL when both match confidences=0.

| # | Column | Type | Nullable | Notes |
|---|--------|------|----------|-------|
| 1 | `business_id` | VARCHAR | Y | UUID — joins across all tables |
| 2 | `primary_naics_code` | INTEGER | Y | Pipeline B output: CASE WHEN zi_match_confidence > efx_match_confidence THEN zi_c_naics6 ELSE efx_primnaicscode |
| 3 | `mcc_code` | VARCHAR | Y |  |
| 4 | `mcc_desc` | VARCHAR | Y |  |
| 5 | `worth_score` | FLOAT | Y | Computed Worth Score (0–1000) — primary risk signal |
| 6 | `match_confidence` | FLOAT | Y |  |
| 7 | `zi_match_confidence` | FLOAT | Y | Pipeline B: zi_match_confidence vs efx_match_confidence — higher wins |
| 8 | `efx_match_confidence` | FLOAT | Y | Pipeline B: zi_match_confidence vs efx_match_confidence — higher wins |
| 9 | `company_name` | VARCHAR | Y |  |
| 10 | `tax_id` | VARCHAR | Y |  |
| 11 | `employee_count` | INTEGER | Y |  |
| 12 | `year_established` | INTEGER | Y |  |
| 13 | `name_verification` | VARCHAR | Y |  |
| 14 | `address_verification` | VARCHAR | Y |  |
| 15 | `tin_verification` | VARCHAR | Y |  |
| 16 | `watchlist_verification` | VARCHAR | Y |  |
| 17 | `primary_naics_description` | VARCHAR | Y |  |

---

### `datascience.zoominfo_matches_custom_inc_ml`

> ZoomInfo entity-match results. zi_probability = XGBoost score. similarity_index 0–55; confidence = index/55. Threshold: similarity_index>=45 AND probability>=0.80.

| # | Column | Type | Nullable | Notes |
|---|--------|------|----------|-------|
| 1 | `business_id` | VARCHAR | Y | UUID — joins across all tables |
| 2 | `zi_c_company_id` | BIGINT | Y |  |
| 3 | `zi_c_location_id` | BIGINT | Y |  |
| 4 | `zi_es_location_id` | VARCHAR | Y |  |
| 5 | `zi_probability` | FLOAT | Y | XGBoost entity-match confidence 0.0–1.0 |
| 6 | `similarity_index` | INTEGER | Y | 0–55 integer; confidence = similarity_index / 55.0 (MAX_CONFIDENCE_INDEX=55 in sources.ts) |

---

### `zoominfo.comp_standard_global`

> ZoomInfo firmographic bulk data. zi_c_naics6 = primary NAICS (6-digit). zi_c_naics_top3 = pipe-delimited top-3 NAICS codes.

|---|--------|------|----------|-------|

| 12 | `zi_c_phone` | VARCHAR | Y |  |
| 13 | `zi_c_naics6` | VARCHAR | Y | ZoomInfo 6-digit NAICS code — primary source for Pipeline B ZI winner |
| 14 | `zi_c_naics_top3` | VARCHAR | Y |  |
| 15 | `zi_c_naics_confidence_score` | FLOAT | Y |  |
| 16 | `zi_c_sic4` | VARCHAR | Y |  |
| 17 | `zi_c_year_founded` | VARCHAR | Y |  |
| 18 | `zi_c_ein` | VARCHAR | Y |  |
| 19 | `zi_c_industry_primary` | VARCHAR | Y |  |

### `warehouse.oc_companies_latest`

> OpenCorporates company registry. industry_code_uids = pipe-delimited codes (format: 'us_naics-722511|uk_sic-56101'). jurisdiction_code = 'us_fl', 'gb', etc.

| # | Column | Type | Nullable | Notes |
|---|--------|------|----------|-------|

| 7 | `incorporation_date` | VARCHAR | Y |  |
| 8 | `dissolution_date` | VARCHAR | Y |  |
| 9 | `industry_code_uids` | VARCHAR | Y | Pipe-delimited: 'us_naics-722511|uk_sic-56101' — parse with SPLIT_PART or Python split |
| 10 | `number_of_employees` | VARCHAR | Y |  |
| 11 | `inactive` | BOOLEAN | Y |  |
| 12 | `has_been_liquidated` | BOOLEAN | Y |  |

## Confirmed Working SQL Patterns

### Fetch any KYB fact:
```sql
SELECT name,
       JSON_EXTRACT_PATH_TEXT(value, 'value')                AS fact_value,
       JSON_EXTRACT_PATH_TEXT(value, 'source', 'platformId') AS winning_vendor,
       JSON_EXTRACT_PATH_TEXT(value, 'source', 'confidence') AS confidence,
       received_at
FROM rds_warehouse_public.facts
WHERE business_id = 'YOUR-UUID'
  AND name IN ('naics_code', 'mcc_code', 'business_name', 'tin_match_boolean');
```

### Pipeline B winner lookup (Redshift):
```sql
SELECT business_id, primary_naics_code, mcc_code, worth_score,
       zi_match_confidence, efx_match_confidence,
       CASE WHEN zi_match_confidence > efx_match_confidence
            THEN 'ZoomInfo (pid=24)' ELSE 'Equifax (pid=17)'
       END AS pipeline_b_winner
FROM datascience.customer_files
WHERE business_id = 'YOUR-UUID';
```

### Parse alternatives array (Python, works for all databases):
```python
import psycopg2, json
cur.execute("SELECT value FROM rds_warehouse_public.facts"
            " WHERE business_id=%s AND name='naics_code'", (business_id,))
row = cur.fetchone()
if row:
    fact = json.loads(row[0])
    primary  = fact.get('value')              # winning code
    vendor   = fact.get('source', {}).get('platformId')  # 17, 24, etc.
    alts     = fact.get('alternatives', [])   # list of {code, confidence, platformId}
```

### Discover new tables at any time:
```sql
-- Run this to see all tables in all Worth AI schemas:
SELECT table_schema, table_name, table_type
FROM information_schema.tables
WHERE table_schema IN ('rds_warehouse_public','rds_cases_public',
                        'integration_data','datascience','zoominfo','warehouse')
  AND table_type = 'BASE TABLE'
ORDER BY table_schema, table_name;
```

### How to update this file after schema changes:
```bash
# Set credentials then run:
export REDSHIFT_HOST='your-cluster.redshift.amazonaws.com'
export REDSHIFT_DBNAME='dev'
export REDSHIFT_USER='your_username'
export REDSHIFT_PASSWORD='your_password'
python3 Admin-Portal-KYB-App/sync_redshift_schema.py

# Then rebuild RAG index:
cd Admin-Portal-KYB-App && python3 kyb_rag_builder.py
git add api-docs/redshift-schema.md kyb_rag_index.json
git commit -m 'sync: Redshift schema' && git push
```
